text:
Clario von Sunaka (クラリオ・フォン・スナカ, Kurario Fon Sunaka) Hunter × Hunter: Each One's Determination is a runaway princess and side character from the video game Hunter × Hunter: Each One's Determination. She appears in Kurapika's route.

Appearance

Clario bears a striking resemblance to Kurapika. She is depicted with blonde bobbed hair, pale skin, pink lipstick, and brown eyes. On her left ear is a pink star earring. Her build is thin. When in public, she adorns a purple cloak featuring red segments. Underneath is a green long-sleeve. In her royal attire, she bears a white frilly dress with pink segments, a matching flower bow holding back her side bangs, white gloves, and pink pearl earrings. 

Personality

She wanders about in Sunaka's streets with a carefree outlook and curiosity. Her intrigue with the outside world is her main attribute throughout her interactions, although she may occasionally be ignorant such as her tendency to dine and dash. Because of her upbringing, she typically acts meek and uncoordinated around others besides Tron. Clario has a habit of apologizing, to the point of saying sorry four consecutive times in a row. She also tends to be gluttonous, with an optional scene revealing she acted as such even before running away. teapotcutie "Hunter X Hunter: Sorezore no Ketsui / Each One's Determination - Missing Person" Youtube 

Background

Clario is a princess involved in an upcoming party at the hotel. Her home life was said to be very strict. Seeking freedom and to be able to explore the outside world, she sneaks away without considering the royal family's servants would try tracking her down. Tron resorts to finding her before the party. 

Cyber Mobile

 Original Japanese Text=
 
クラピカそっくりなオヒメサマ。
緋の眼には変わらない。
さいきんおしろをよくぬけ出していらしい。
 

|-|Translated English Text=
 
A princess who resembles Kurapika.
She doesn't have Scarlet Eyes.
She seems to have recently escaped into the outskirts of Sunaka.
 
 

Plot

Each One's Determination

 Note: Events occurring in Each One's Determination do not constitute canon material. 

While Kurapika is searching for the Sengi Guild, the Kurta member explores Sunaka's streets for clues. He soon enters a local hotel. There, the receptionist makes mention of the town's princess and an upcoming party. A few residents also make mention of the police going after a royal figure. Entering the alleyways, he bumps into a cloaked figure. Said figure is of Clario herself. A brief discussion occurs where she apologizes and is left shocked by Kurapika's appearance. Later, he notes the girl running about in the streets with a bag filled with burgers. As Kurapika continues going through Sunaka, he is soon mistaken for an individual at Burger Kong who's been dine-and-dashing, resulting in him running away from the crowd after him. 

As he hides, Kurapika once more runs into the cloaked princess in the alleyways. She begs him to help her right as two goons appear. As they confront him, Clario herself hides. A mysterious man named Tron then finds Kurapika. He mistakes him for Clario. Kurapika attempts to fight off the suited man, only to be defeated by him instantly. His Scarlet Eyes are soon triggered. This results in Tron and his subordinates leaving. Noting them to be alone, Kurapika demands the other reveal her face to him and lifts her hood away. Kurapika is promptly left shocked by their resemblance to each other. Clario then explains herself, resulting in Kurapika taking her to Burger Kong to apologize. As they head there, the duo is soon encountered by the Burger Kong owner, where the situation is dealt with. It is revealed that the police paid off the fines. However, Kurapika demands that Clario still apologize for what she did. After she does so, the duo eat together. 

Later that day, they arrive at the hotel. From his hunter license, Kurapika is able to give Clario her own room. She then visits him in the night, asking if there's anything she can do for him. He inquires if she knows anything regarding Tron's power. She is unable to answer. Kurapika then asks if Tron is a hunter. She comments no, but he mentioned being trained by one. As the duo talk, it is revealed that Clario knows of Rorgo and the Scarlet Eyes. However, she refuses to comment further. In the morning, a note is left behind in her room, revealing her to have ran away. 

Later on, the runaway is found by herself in a nearby alley. She claims to have been starving herself from not being given food. Kurapika asks if she will follow him. A hungry Clario only agrees only if brought out to eat once more. After they eat, Kurapika questions why she doesn't want to return home. She comments that it's too strict there, feeling she can't even move around there. The princess then claims to be jealous of Kurapika, who became a hunter by his own choice. This results in Kurapika becoming upset. They soon return to the hotel room again, where Kurapika calls Tron. Clario then suddenly escapes. Kurapika is forced to chase her throughout the hotel until they reach its storage warehouse. There, she suddenly notes a spider. With the other left distracted, she manages to escape. He is forced to take her place at the party. During this, Kurapika later notes her to be hiding in the cake area and tells Tron. This results in an escaping Clario accidentally running into Rorgo and accidentally tearing his shirt. Tron's men then take her away. 

Later on, as Kurapika is training with Izunavi, he suddenly receives a call from Tron. It is revealed that Clario is missing. Kurapika later concludes with Tron that Rorgo abducted her, mistaking the girl as him. He then proceeds to track down his location. Using his newfound chain abilities, he goes to his mansion to rescue her. Kurapika soon finds Clario locked in a room. She claims that after Rorgo and his men realized her true identity, they decided to keep her alive and interrogated her. Kurapika proceeds to release her, only for a trap to trigger. The building then begins collapsing as they escape. 

As they are running away, Rorgo himself appears. Kurapika attempts to use Chain Jail, but due to it lacking its restrictions, Rorgo is left unaffected. Suddenly, Izunavi and Tron appear. Rorgo soon escapes. Clario and Tron later thank Kurapika at the hotel. As he begins to leave, she questions if they will meet again. He refuses to respond. After Kurapika becomes a part of the Nostrade Family, Tron escorts Clario to Sunaka, where they plan to meet with him. She soon runs into Kurapika. He reveals he plans to say goodbye to her one last time. Once more, she questions if they will meet again. He says no and wishes for her safety, leaving Clario to cry out to him. 

Trivia

Her Japanese name is a pun on Kurapika's in reference to their similar appearances.
Clario uses the personal pronoun 'watakushi,' which is associated with higher-class people and formal language.
In-game, she has a different eye color than Kurapika, who is depicted with green instead, except when disguised as her.
With that said, the duo are the only properly named characters to share the same sprite, said sprite being Clario's formal wear.
Clario's full name is not revealed in her in-game character description, instead stated by Tron and at the VIP party.
Tron briefly mentions her having a cell phone in Kurapika's sixth chapter.

Intertextuality and References

Clario shares similarities to Neon from the original series. Both are teenagers of high status who rely on Kurapika for assistance. Both also sneak off from their bodyguards/servants to pursue their own goals.
Incidentally, Neon appears in Gon and Killua's route.
Her role in the story appears to be loosely based on the Mark Twain novel The Prince and the Pauper.
Clario claims her favorite food is cake. This appears to be a reference to Marie Antoinette, the last queen of France before the French Revolution. She is commonly misattributed to saying the phrase "Let them eat cake," which was in response to the country's food crisis.
